claim: Most AI agent harness components (detailed planning, context isolation, sprint contracts) are now dead weight with Opus 4.6; each component encodes outdated assumptions about model capability
actionable: Strip unnecessary harness components; use only planner, generator, and evaluator agents; eliminate micro-detailed planning and context resets
source_title: Anthropic Just Killed All Your Agent Harnesses

claim: Micro-detailed planning (task sharding in B-Mad, Speckit) now causes cascading failures in Opus 4.6; high-level planning with product-level scope is more effective
actionable: Move from implementation-level planning to product-level planning; let Opus discover implementation details rather than micro-specifying them
source_title: Anthropic Just Killed All Your Agent Harnesses

claim: Self-evaluation is problematic; the same agent that wrote code will confidently praise its own work even when quality is poor; separate evaluator agents are essential
actionable: Always use separate evaluator agents; never have the generator evaluate its own code
source_title: Anthropic Just Killed All Your Agent Harnesses

claim: With Opus 4.6, sprint contracts (pre-negotiated specs between generator and evaluator) are no longer necessary; generator capability is sufficient for execution without formal contracts
actionable: Remove sprint contracts for Opus 4.6; for smaller models (Sonnet, Haiku) still use contracts and detailed task breakdown
source_title: Anthropic Just Killed All Your Agent Harnesses

claim: Context anxiety (models wrapping up prematurely as context fills) is eliminated in Opus 4.5+; context resets and detailed task breakdowns are no longer necessary
actionable: Remove context reset patterns for Opus 4.5+; let agents run continuously across sessions; rely on built-in compaction
source_title: Anthropic Just Killed All Your Agent Harnesses

claim: Graded evaluation with scored rubrics (not just pass/fail) ensures the evaluator knows what right output looks like; critical for subjective tasks like UI design
actionable: Create scoring rubrics for UI design, code architecture, UX; use graded evaluation instead of binary checks
source_title: Anthropic Just Killed All Your Agent Harnesses

claim: For UI evaluation: quality (coherence), originality (avoid purple-white gradient defaults), craft (typography, spacing, contrast), and functionality are key scoring criteria
actionable: Specify UI scoring criteria explicitly; push models beyond default patterns through evaluation guidance
source_title: Anthropic Just Killed All Your Agent Harnesses

claim: Use agent teams with dedicated generator and evaluator roles; they can communicate directly (unlike sub-agents) and coordinate on issues in real time
actionable: Use Claude agent teams (not sub-agents) for generator-evaluator pairs; enable real-time feedback loops
source_title: Anthropic Just Killed All Your Agent Harnesses
